sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content:
{
  "role": "Primary local therapy for non-metastatic breast cancer with curative intent. May also be used for palliation in metastatic disease.",
  "principles": [
    "The choice of surgery is based on patient preference, tumor size, tumor location, breast size, and contraindications to radiation.",
    "Breast-conserving surgery (BCS) is equivalent to mastectomy with regard to overall survival for stage I and II breast cancer (category 1).",
    "Achieving pathologically negative margins is essential for BCS. For invasive cancer, a positive margin is defined as 'ink on tumor'.",
    "Axillary lymph node staging is critical for prognosis and treatment planning. Sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB) is the preferred method for axillary staging in clinically node-negative patients.",
    "Reconstruction options should be discussed with all patients undergoing mastectomy. Reconstruction does not impact oncologic outcomes."
  ],
  "resectability_criteria": "For BCS, absolute contraindications include: inflammatory breast cancer or extensive skin/dermal lymphatic involvement; diffuse suspicious microcalcifications; inability to achieve negative margins (defined as no ink on tumor); homozygous ATM mutation (category 2B); gestational breast cancer unable to receive RT within 12–16 weeks; and multicentric disease with certain criteria (category 2B). Relative contraindications include: known genetic predisposition, TP53 PV, active connective tissue disease, prior RT to the area, and pregnancy.",
  "pre_operative_optimisation": "Accurate clinical staging prior to surgery is critical. For patients considering preoperative systemic therapy, additional workup includes axillary assessment (ultrasound ± biopsy), breast MRI (optional), and consideration of gene expression assays. Coordination with plastic surgery for reconstruction planning should occur prior to surgery."
}

cards.name: Breast-Conserving Surgery (BCS)
cards.detail:
{
  "name": "Breast-Conserving Surgery (BCS)",
  "approach": "Lumpectomy or partial mastectomy with goal of achieving negative margins. Oncoplastic techniques may be used for optimal cosmesis.",
  "indication": "Most patients with early-stage invasive breast cancer or DCIS who are candidates for adjuvant radiation therapy.",
  "key_complications": "Positive margins requiring re-excision, infection, poor cosmesis.",
  "operative_mortality": "Low"
}

cards.name: Mastectomy
cards.detail:
{
  "name": "Mastectomy",
  "approach": "Total, skin-sparing, or nipple-sparing mastectomy with surgical axillary staging. Delayed or immediate reconstruction may be performed.",
  "indication": "Patients not candidates for BCS, those with contraindications to RT, or patient preference.",
  "key_complications": "Surgical site infection, bleeding, seroma, poor cosmesis, complications of reconstruction (capsular contracture, implant failure, flap necrosis).",
  "operative_mortality": "Low"
}

cards.name: Axillary Lymph Node Staging (SLNB vs. ALND)
cards.detail:
{
  "name": "Axillary Lymph Node Staging (SLNB vs. ALND)",
  "approach": "SLNB is the preferred method for staging clinically node-negative patients. ALND (Level I/II) is reserved for patients with clinically suspicious nodes or positive SLNB not meeting criteria for omission.",
  "indication": "Standard staging procedure for most patients with invasive breast cancer undergoing surgery.",
  "key_complications": "Lymphedema, shoulder stiffness, numbness, seroma.",
  "operative_mortality": "Low"
}
